title: Verify Parcel Data Elements Available As A Parcel Reaches The Sorter
summary: Use the source-described parcel lifecycle and Chat message content to verify which parcel data elements should be available by the time a parcel is on the sorter. The source states that parcels move from belts leading to sorter induction, then a first Chat message provides tracking ID, dimensions, and package type, and a second Chat message provides tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data. At that point, the parcel is on the sorter with dimensional data and an indication of where it could be going.
when_to_use: Use this runbook when you need to confirm, based on the training source, which parcel data elements should be present by the time a parcel reaches sorter context.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer additional parcel fields beyond the source-listed data elements.
Do not use this runbook to determine system navigation, commands, or screen workflows not provided by the source.
Do not use this runbook to assign actual final destination logic beyond the source statement that there is potential destination data and an indication of where the parcel could be going.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the source-described parcel lifecycle and verify whether the expected source-listed parcel data elements are present.

safety_notes: This is a source-based verification/reference procedure and is marked support safe in the candidate.
Do not infer additional fields, destinations, or system actions beyond the data elements explicitly named in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to parcel data or message information
Source documentation or training slide describing parcel lifecycle and Chat messages

access_required: Access to parcel data or message information
Access to the source documentation or training slide describing parcel lifecycle and Chat messages
system_preconditions: A parcel is being evaluated at the point described by the source as reaching sorter context.

visual_references.description: Training slide describing the parcel lifecycle before and at sorter induction, including the first and second Chat messages and the statement that the parcel is on the sorter with dimensional data and an indication of where it could be going.

steps.title: Review the parcel lifecycle up to sorter entry
steps.purpose: Establish the source-defined point in the parcel lifecycle where the verification should be performed.
steps.instruction: Follow the source-described parcel lifecycle from parcels being unloaded from trailers onto belts leading to sorter induction to the point where the parcel is on the sorter.
steps.expected_result: You identify the source-defined lifecycle point at which parcel data should be checked.
steps.healthy_condition: The parcel is being evaluated at the source-described point where it is on the sorter.
steps.failure_condition: The parcel is being evaluated at a different lifecycle point than the one described in the source, so the expected data set cannot be confirmed against this reference.

actions.label: Review lifecycle reference
actions.exact_action: Follow the source-described parcel lifecycle from belts leading to sorter induction to the point where the parcel is on the sorter.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The lifecycle summary showing parcels unloaded from trailers onto belts leading to sorter induction and the parcel state at sorter entry.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if you cannot align the parcel being reviewed to the source-described sorter-entry point.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Before parcels reach our system, they are unloaded from trailers in bulk onto a series of belts that lead to sorter induction.

steps.title: Check first Chat message data elements
steps.purpose: Verify the initial source-listed parcel attributes that should be available in the first Chat message.
steps.instruction: Check for the first Chat message data elements listed in the source: tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.
steps.expected_result: You can confirm whether tracking ID, dimensions, and package type are present in the first Chat message context.
steps.healthy_condition: The first Chat message includes tracking ID, dimensions, and package type as described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of the source-listed first-message data elements are missing or do not align with the source description.

actions.label: Verify first message fields
actions.exact_action: Check for the first Chat message data elements listed in the source: tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text listing the first Chat message fields: tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the available parcel data does not align with the source-described first message content.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This triggers the first message from Chat with a tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.

steps.title: Check second Chat message data elements
steps.purpose: Verify the additional source-listed parcel attributes that should be available in the second Chat message.
steps.instruction: Check for the second Chat message data elements listed in the source: tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
steps.expected_result: You can confirm whether tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data are present in the second Chat message context.
steps.healthy_condition: The second Chat message includes tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data as described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of the source-listed second-message data elements are missing or do not align with the source description.

actions.label: Verify second message fields
actions.exact_action: Check for the second Chat message data elements listed in the source: tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text listing the second Chat message fields: tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the available parcel data does not align with the source-described second message content.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This triggers the second message from Chat with a tracking Id, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

steps.title: Compare observed data to sorter-ready source statement
steps.purpose: Confirm that the observed parcel data matches the source statement for the parcel state when it is on the sorter.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed parcel data to the source statement that, at this point, the parcel is on the sorter and there is dimensional data plus an indication of where it could be going.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the observed parcel data matches the source-described sorter-ready state.
steps.healthy_condition: The parcel is on the sorter with dimensional data and an indication of where it could be going, consistent with the source.
steps.failure_condition: The observed parcel data does not support the source-described sorter-ready state.

actions.label: Compare to sorter-ready state
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed parcel data to the source statement that, at this point, the parcel is on the sorter and there is dimensional data plus an indication of where it could be going.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide statement that by this point the parcel is on the sorter with dimensional data and an indication of where it could be going.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed parcel data does not align with the source-described sorter-ready state.
Stop if verification would require inferring additional fields, destinations, or system actions not explicitly named in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At that point the parcel is on the sorter with dimensional data and an indication of where it could be going.

steps.title: Record present and missing source-listed data elements
steps.purpose: Document the verification result using only source-supported field names.
steps.instruction: Record which expected data elements are present and which are missing using only the source-provided field names.
steps.expected_result: A source-grounded record exists showing which expected parcel data elements are present and which are missing.
steps.healthy_condition: The record uses only the source-listed field names and clearly distinguishes present versus missing elements.
steps.failure_condition: The record includes inferred fields, inferred destination logic, or unsupported terminology not present in the source.

actions.label: Document verification result
actions.exact_action: Record which expected data elements are present and which are missing using only the source-provided field names.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if documenting the result would require adding field names not present in the source.
Escalate if the available parcel data does not align with the source-described first and second message content.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This triggers the first message from Chat with a tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.
This triggers the second message from Chat with a tracking Id, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

success_criteria: The user can verify whether the source-described parcel data set is available by the time the parcel reaches the sorter.
The verification result distinguishes the source-listed first and second Chat message data elements.
The result is documented using only source-provided field names.
healthy_conditions: The parcel is evaluated at the source-described point where it is on the sorter.
The first Chat message data elements are available as tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.
The second Chat message data elements are available as tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
The parcel has dimensional data and an indication of where it could be going, consistent with the source.
failure_conditions: Available parcel data does not align with the source-described first message content.
Available parcel data does not align with the source-described second message content.
Verification requires inferring additional fields, destinations, or system actions beyond the source.
The parcel is not being evaluated at the source-described sorter-entry point.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the available parcel data does not align with the source-described first and second message content.
Stop and escalate if verification would require inferring additional fields, destinations, or system actions beyond the data elements explicitly named in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Parcel lifecycle before sorter induction, first Chat message fields, second Chat message fields, and the sorter-ready parcel state.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: This triggers the first message from Chat with a tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.
This triggers the second message from Chat with a tracking Id, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
Before parcels reach our system, they are unloaded from trailers in bulk onto a series of belts that lead to sorter induction.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact system screens or navigation steps for where to inspect parcel data.
The source does not provide commands, API calls, or terminal actions.
The source does not define a formal recording template for documenting present versus missing fields.
The source does not specify timing estimates for completing this verification.
The source does not define explicit role boundaries beyond the candidate's likely role requirement.
